Evidence source: CCEM pair tests and threshold sensitivity
Supported interpretation: Pet retail and pet medical services co-locate above matched expectations.
Interpretation limit: The evidence does not show that pet services meet demand or improve welfare.

Supported interpretation: The matched design identifies co-location beyond conventional urban context.
Interpretation limit: The analysis cannot separate service-system bundling from shared unobserved pet ownership or household pet demand.

Supported interpretation: Non-spatial specificity is consistently benchmark-leading; spatial specificity is strong but not universal.
Interpretation limit: The pet coupling is not interpreted as always the strongest service-pair relationship.

Supported interpretation: Coupling remains above the upper-tail null threshold in all 40 spatial tests.
Interpretation limit: Spatial tests reduce, but do not eliminate, spatial confounding.

Supported interpretation: The pet-medical POI layer is strongly validated.
Interpretation limit: The layer is not a complete census of veterinary care.

Supported interpretation: The pet-retail layer is supportive but caveated.
Interpretation limit: The layer is not treated as perfectly classified.

Supported interpretation: Elder-care POI is too fragile for main empirical claims.
Interpretation limit: The elder-care layer is not used to support a substantive all-age care contrast.

Supported interpretation: Local panels support face validity of diagnostic signatures.
Interpretation limit: Local panels do not prove use, demand, quality or accessibility.

Supported interpretation: Conventional variables partially recover the with-pet typology.
Interpretation limit: The model is not interpreted as strongly predicting or validating all signature classes.
